title: Mildly Sweet Saudi Arabian Pasta Pilaf
description: A tangy and crisp Saudi Arabian dish featuring a unique pasta water emulsion, this pilaf is perfect for a romantic evening, and can be easily adapted to be sugar free friendly.
instructions: Bring the chicken broth to a boil in a large pot.
Add the pasta to the pot and cook until al dente.
While the pasta is cooking, heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Stir the tomato paste into the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Drain the pasta and add it to the skillet, stirring to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Add the granulated sugar, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the pilaf for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
